description_long: This object is a detailed model of a NASA Space Shuttle orbiter, featuring a streamlined white fuselage with black thermal protection tiles along the nose, wing edges, and underbelly. The wings are delta-shaped and low-mounted, with a slight upward dihedral and black leading edges. The rear section houses three large main engines, two smaller orbital maneuvering system (OMS) pods, and a vertical stabilizer with a black tip. The cockpit is located at the forward end, with a distinctive black windshield and window arrangement. The landing gear is partially visible, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear under the cockpit. The overall appearance is sleek and aerodynamic, designed for both atmospheric flight and space re-entry.
description: A white NASA Space Shuttle orbiter with black-tipped delta wings, three rear engines, and a forward cockpit with black windows.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full delta wing shape, black leading edges, and the forward cockpit windows.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the wing profile, and the rear engine cluster.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the three main engines, OMS pods, and the vertical stabilizer.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the opposite wing and the detailed rear section.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-mounted delta wings with slight upward dihedral and black leading edges; no winglets present.
attribute_engine_type: Three rear-mounted jet engines with two additional OMS pods flanking the main engines.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and no horizontal stabilizer extensions beyond the wing.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear unit.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with a black-framed windshield and side windows.
